name: QNI : Same Threat Detected on Multiple Hosts
notes: This rule triggers when the same file hash associated with malware is seen being transferred to multiple destinations, which indicates that malware is spreading within the network.
conditions_text: when QNI : Observed File Hash Associated with Malware Threat match at least 20 times with the same File Hash (QNI) and different Destination IP in 15 minutes
thresholds: when QNI : Observed File Hash Associated with Malware Threat match at least 20 times with the same File Hash (QNI) and different Destination IP in 15 minutes
TD_list: APPLY QNI : Same Threat Detected on Multiple Hosts on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when QNI : Observed File Hash Associated with Malware Threat match at least 20 times with the same File Hash (QNI) and different Destination IP in 15 minutes
RED_list: Event name: QNI : Same Threat Detected on Multiple Hosts
Event description: Detects when the same file hash associated with malware is seen being transferred to multiple destinations.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
